Pre-Shift · Crown Harbor Emergency Operations
NIGHT 7 — OPERATIONAL BRIEFING
SUPERVISOR ·
The Plan
Advancement & Experience (click to expand / collapse) [ COLLAPSE ]
Unit Roster — Advancement Status
NEW CAPABILITY — MARSHAL — FORTIFY PROTOCOL Your newly advanced Police unit can run a Fortify operation on any controlled district. While the Marshal holds position, organized assaults are stopped at the perimeter — regular calls still come through. Use Fortify to protect your HQ under heavy pressure or hold a reclaimed district while you push elsewhere. You only have one Marshal tonight. Use them deliberately and keep an eye on their fatigue while deployed.
TONIGHT'S FOCUS
⬡ ALL COMM CHANNELS IN USE
You cannot dispatch another unit until a call clears.
You currently have active channels.
⚠ HQ PERIMETER — WITHDRAWAL
"Give that order to pull back and we are all dead, there will be nothing left in their way. Nothing."
⚠ ESCORT — WITHDRAW MARSHAL
Collection is still in progress. Pulling the Marshal now leaves the Specialist exposed in hostile territory.

50/50 — Specialist retreats or is lost.
Sewer Crew — Entry Refused
Docklands / Line 7 Access Point
Field Report
Your Decision
⬡ {dispatchedUnit} — HOLDING AT ACCESS POINT
Your crew is at the access point and refusing to go further. They say something smells wrong and there's movement below the junction. Could be anything — gas pocket, animals, runoff from Foundry Ward. They want to come back up.
Docklands — Unit Dark
PW Unit — Last contact: Docklands access road
Last Known Transmissions
Your Decision
⬡ UNIT DARK — NO CONTACT
Your unit went dark at the Docklands perimeter road twenty minutes ago. No signal, no callback. The last thing they radioed was that the fence line had been breached. You have three options. None of them are good.
—
—
Incoming Report
⚠ Triage — Ask Caller for Intel
You are the dispatcher. Ask the caller for information before committing units. Each question reveals more — but the clock is running. On this shift, caller comms are reliable.

Keyboard Shortcuts
Control playback without the mouse

Unit Panel Stack

Comm Channel Display Position
Click a position on the screen diagram to move the Comm Channels box. Default: bottom-left.
Critical Alert Position
Click a position on the screen diagram to move where critical alerts appear. Default: top-center.
Community Trust

Trust measures public confidence in your dispatch operation. It is your primary performance metric — each shift is graded on it at debrief. Effective responses build trust; failed calls, ignored incidents, and poor decisions erode it. Starting trust: 67% (10 of 15).

EventChange
SEV 1 Resolved+0.5
SEV 2 Resolved+1
SEV 3+ Resolved+2
Incident Failed−1
Forced Entry — Scene Unsecured−2
Timeout — SEV 1 or 2−1
Timeout — SEV 3+−2
Declined / Recalled / Withdrawn — SEV 1 or 2−0.5
Declined / Recalled / Withdrawn — SEV 3+−1
Smart Triage Recall ②0
Unit Lost in Action−1
Scripted Narrative Event0
Night End — Districts Held ① (Night 5+)Held × 0.1

① Night End Bonus: awarded per controlled district at shift end (max 10 districts reclaimed). Scales with how much of the city you hold.

② Smart Triage: unit recalled while all channels are full or all units are deployed, and a higher-priority response is dispatched within 20 seconds.

Unit Advancement

Units earn XP by successfully resolving any mission: SEV 1 = 1 XP, SEV 2 = 2 XP, SEV 3 = 3 XP, SEV 4 = 4 XP, SEV 5 = 5 XP. Advancing to L2 requires 10 XP and unlocks that unit's strategic mission capability. L3 requires 26 total XP. Higher tiers reduce fatigue drain and improve mission success rates across all operations.

TypeL1 — BaseL2 — 10 XPL3 — 26 XP
CAPABILITIES · Base stats
· Can lead Sev 1–3		 · −15% fatigue drain
· +8% success same-class
· Unlocks Sev 4 missions		 · −25% fatigue drain
· +15% success all
· Unlocks Sev 5 missions
